summary: High‑quality soundboard capture of a solid ’93 Spring show, highlighted by a scorching Bertha opener and a soaring Eyes of the World.
review: The recording is widely praised for its clarity and faithful mix, with only a minor note about Jerry’s wah tone being low in the blend. Musically the concert delivers several standouts – Bertha, Eyes of the World, Space, Lazy River Road and Queen Jane – while a few numbers (Wang‑Dang‑Doodle, Loose Lucy) are seen as average. Overall fans describe the performance as an above‑average set that ebbs and flows in classic Dead fashion.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4–5 stars, lauding both the show’s musical peaks and the clean soundboard source; only a handful of minor criticisms keep it from the top tier.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix praised by many reviewers; one comment notes Jerry’s wah on "Greatest Story" sits low in the blend but does not detract from overall fidelity.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows with a strong opening (Bertha) and a second‑set that many describe as above average, though the performance ebbs and flows—peaking on extended jams and dipping on a few pedestrian numbers.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His wah tone on "Greatest Story" is noted as low in the mix but his overall playing is praised.
